heading: Covered vessels
label: 3802 Covered vessels
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this title”, meaning title X of , , , which enacted this chapter and repealed chapter 37 (§ 2401 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of title X to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 111–281124 Stat. 3023Oct. 15, 2010
amendments: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this title”, meaning title X of , , , which enacted this chapter and repealed chapter 37 (§ 2401 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of title X to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 111–281124 Stat. 3023Oct. 15, 2010

children.text: A vessel documented under chapter 121 of title 46 or one operated under the authority of the United States, wherever located.
Any vessel permitted by a Federal agency to operate on the Outer Continental Shelf.

children.label: A
children.text: in the internal waters of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: in any port, shipyard, offshore terminal, or other place in the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: lightering in the territorial sea; or

children.label: D
children.text: to the extent consistent with international law, anchoring in the territorial sea of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: Any warship, naval auxiliary, or other vessel owned or operated by a foreign state, and used, for the time being, only on government noncommercial service.

children.label: B
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), any warship, naval auxiliary, or other vessel owned or operated by the United States and used for the time being only on government noncommercial service.

children.heading: Application to United States government vessels
children.label: 2 Application to United States government vessels

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator may apply any requirement of this chapter to one or more classes of vessels described in paragraph (1)(B), if the head of the Federal department or agency under which those vessels operate concurs in that application.

children.heading: Limitation for combat-related vessel
children.label: B Limitation for combat-related vessel
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to combat-related vessels.
